title: Diagnose unexplained pod restarts under traffic
prompt:
Our `api-server` pod has been restarting unexpectedly during peak traffic for the past two days. The pod has a memory limit of 2Gi, and it usually idles around 800MB. There are no recent code deploys. Here's the dmesg snippet from the worker node around the last restart — some lines have been redacted because the operator pasted only the parts they thought were relevant:

```
[Sun Apr 19 14:32:01 2026] api-server invoked [REDACTED]: gfp_mask=0x100cca(GFP_HIGHUSER_MOVABLE), order=0
[Sun Apr 19 14:32:01 2026] CPU: 3 PID: 28475 Comm: api-server
[Sun Apr 19 14:32:01 2026] Memory cgroup [REDACTED]: Killed process 28475 (api-server) total-vm:3284812kB, anon-rss:2097148kB
[Sun Apr 19 14:32:01 2026] reaped process 28475 (api-server), now anon-rss:0kB
```

What happened, what likely caused it (give your top hypotheses), and what concrete next steps should the on-call engineer take to diagnose and remediate? Be specific about what you'd measure or change first.
